notes:
Recipe Generator: You can now create recipes right inside OrcaSheets. A guided multi-step dialog walks you through the entire process — select your source sheets, describe what you want to analyze, and set preferences like whether to generate a dashboard or how many insights to include. OrcaSheets sends your sheet metadata to AI, which produces a complete recipe structure. Every SQL step is then validated locally against your actual data before anything runs, so you know exactly what will work and what needs tweaking. The results are shown as an interactive flowchart — a visual map of your recipe's data flow with expandable steps, dependency graphs, color-coded pass/fail indicators, and output type badges. Failed steps auto-expand so you can see what went wrong immediately. If something's off, just describe what you'd like changed in plain language, and the recipe regenerates and re-validates. Once everything passes, hit Publish to submit it for review.

 Recipe Flowchart Visualization: Generated recipes are displayed as a visual dependency graph — each step is an expandable card showing its SQL logic, validation status, output type (sheet, aggregation, visualization, or dashboard), and how it connects to other steps. Green means it passed, red means it failed with error details right there, and orange means it's still validating.

 Recipe Runs Hub: A new Runs tab in RecipeHub gives you a single place to monitor every recipe execution in your project. Running jobs float to the top with a live progress bar and step counter. Completed, failed, and partially-succeeded runs are all listed with status indicators, relative timestamps, and duration. Click any run to view full details, re-run it with its previous parameters, or delete it for a clean removal of all generated artifacts. Closing an active recipe execution now takes you straight to the Runs tab instead of back to the recipe grid.
